pre_execution_steps: 1. HANDS writes data/batch_6_leads.json from Harrogate + Craven scraping
2. BRAIN assigns signals + generates email copy (Mon 2026-06-01 or earlier)
3. Day-0 sends Tue 2026-06-02 — IMAP per lead before each send
4. HANDS replaces all BATCH6_P0xx google_query entries with name-specific queries from batch_6_leads.json
5. Day 3 (Fri 2026-06-05): execute LinkedIn searches + connection requests + write linkedin_profiles.json SAME SESSION
6. orchestrator.py executes all of step 5 automatically — MUST be deployed before 2026-06-05
per_lead_protocol: Read search query from this file (use name-specific query if available, sector-discovery if not)
Google search → extract LinkedIn profile URL → confirm is business owner
Check posts from last 60 days — flag if recent post found (enables post_reference note type)
Read connection note from linkedin_connection_notes.json (match by lead_id)
Send connection request via LinkedIn with note
Write entry to data/linkedin_profiles.json IMMEDIATELY (before moving to next lead)
Log errors and skip reasons — never crash silently
batch_6_execution_protocol.archive_trigger_rule: If linkedin_profiles.json does not contain at least one entry by Tue 2026-06-10 EOD — all Batch 6 leads archived LOST_NO_RESPONSE on LinkedIn. No exceptions. No extensions.

orchestrator_status.status: NOT_IMPLEMENTED — src/outlocal/linkedin/ directory does not exist
orchestrator_status.build_urgency: CRITICAL — 63-session manual delivery failure cannot repeat. orchestrator.py is the ONLY solution that guarantees same-session execution.
minimum_viable_implementation: Drive Playwright: Google search per query → extract LinkedIn profile URL
Confirm profile is business owner (not employee) before proceeding
Check posts tab for posts from last 60 days — flag if found
Read connection note template from data/linkedin_connection_notes.json — match by lead_id
Drive LinkedIn: send connection request + note (confirm note under 200 chars)
Write entry to data/linkedin_profiles.json IMMEDIATELY after each attempt (not batched)
Log errors and skip reasons — never crash silently
On completion: print summary count (sent / skipped / failed)
data_dependencies: data/linkedin_search_plan.json — source of search queries (this file)
data/linkedin_connection_notes.json — source of connection note templates
data/linkedin_profiles.json — output file (write per lead, not batched)
